Little do people know about the happiness of those who truly follow Christ. Little do they think that these poor, humble, and despised ones are favored by God and will live in heaven. Let followers of Christ be content with simple food here, since they are in a foreign land where their Lord was so badly treated before them. The children of God must walk by faith and live by hope. They may well wait in faith, hope, and eager anticipation for the revealing of the Lord Jesus. The children of God will be known and made evident by being like their leader. They will be transformed into his image through seeing him.

The children of God know that their Lord’s eyes are pure enough to not allow anything unholy or impure to dwell with him. It is the hope of hypocrites, not of the children of God, that allows for gratifying impure desires and lusts. May we be followers of him as his dear children, thus show our sense of his unspeakable mercy, and express that obedient, grateful, humble mind which becomes us. Sin is rejecting the Divine law. In Christ, there was no sin. He took all the harmless weaknesses that were consequences of the fall—that is, all those infirmities of mind or body which subject man to suffering and expose him to temptation. But he did not have our moral weaknesses, our tendency toward sin. Whoever stays in Christ does not continue practicing sin. Renouncing sin is the greatest proof of spiritual union with, continuation in, and saving knowledge of the Lord Christ. Beware of deceiving yourself. Whoever acts righteously is righteous, and being a follower of Christ shows an interest by faith in his obedience and sufferings. But a man cannot act like the devil and at the same time be a disciple of Christ Jesus. Let us not serve or indulge what the Son of God came to destroy. To be born of God is to be inwardly renewed by the power of the Spirit of God. Renewing grace is an abiding principle. Religion is not an art, a matter of dexterity and skill, but a new nature. And the regenerate person cannot sin as he did before being born of God, and as others do who are not born again. There is a light in his mind that shows him the evil and harm of sin. There is a bias upon his heart that disposes him to loathe and hate sin. There is the spiritual principle that opposes sinful acts. And there is repentance for sin if it is committed. It goes against him to sin with forethought. The children of God and the children of the devil have their distinct characters. The seed of the serpent are known by neglect of religion, and by hating real Christians. He only is righteous before God, as a justified believer, who is taught and disposed to righteousness by the Holy Spirit. In this, the children of God are manifest, and the children of the devil. May all professors of the gospel lay these truths to heart and try themselves by them.

We should love the Lord Jesus, value his love, and therefore love all our brothers in Christ. This love is the special fruit of our faith and a certain sign of being born again. But none who rightly know the heart of man can wonder at the contempt and enmity of ungodly people against the children of God. We know that we have passed from death to life: we may know it by the evidences of our faith in Christ, of which love to our brothers is one. It is not zeal for a group within common religion or affection for those who are of the same name and sentiments as ourselves. The life of grace in the heart of a regenerate person is the beginning and first principle of a life of glory, whereof they must be destitute who hate their brother in their hearts.

Here is the humility, the miracle, the mystery of Divine love: God would redeem the church with his own blood. Surely we should love those whom God has loved and so loved. The Holy Spirit, grieved at selfishness, will leave the selfish heart without comfort and full of darkness and terror. By what can it be known that a man has a true sense of the love of Christ for perishing sinners, or that the love of God has been planted in his heart by the Holy Spirit, if the love of the world and its good overcomes the feelings of compassion to a perishing brother? Every instance of this selfishness must weaken the evidences of a man’s conversion; when habitual and allowed, it must decide against him. If conscience condemns us in known sin or the neglect of known duty, God does too. Let conscience therefore be well-informed, be heard, and diligently attended to.

When believers had confidence toward God through the Spirit of adoption and by faith in the great High Priest, they might ask for whatever they would of their reconciled Father. He will give it if it is good for them. And as goodwill to men was proclaimed from heaven, so goodwill to men, particularly to brothers, must be in the hearts of those who go to God and heaven. Whoever thus follows Christ dwells in him as his ark, refuge, and rest, and in the Father through him. This union between Christ and the souls of believers is by the Spirit he has given them. A man may believe that God is gracious before he knows it; yet when faith has laid hold on the promises, it sets reason to work. This Spirit of God works a change; in all true Christians it changes from the power of Satan to the power of God. Consider, believer, how it changes your heart. Do you long for peace with God? Would you forego all the world for it? No profit, pleasure, or preferment shall hinder you from following Christ. This salvation is built upon Divine testimony, even the Spirit of God.
